     r == 0 ? stringfn(s, p^q) : stringfn(s, p^q, first(p, r))
 end
 
+"""
+    rtrunc(str::AbstractString, w::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `w` characters (see [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the last characters
+with `replace_str` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = rtrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 I lo…"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> rtrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+function rtrunc(str::AbstractString, w::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")","path":"base/strings/util.jl","commit_id":"3f012b2cb6a628719bf4c27fe04fb55ce1d8bc45","original_commit_id":"3f012b2cb6a628719bf4c27fe04fb55ce1d8bc45","user":{"login":"stevengj","id":2913679,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTM2Nzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2913679?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj","html_url":"https://github.com/stevengj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion

     r == 0 ? stringfn(s, p^q) : stringfn(s, p^q, first(p, r))
 end
 
+"""
+    rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the last characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = rtrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 I lo…"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> rtrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :right)
+
+"""
+    ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the first characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ltrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"…I love 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ltrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`rtrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :left)
+
+"""
+    ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true)
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the middle characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…". By default, the truncation
+prefers keeping chars on the left, but this can be changed by setting `prefer_left` to `false`.
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ctrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 …e 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ctrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`rtrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true) =
+    struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :center, prefer_left)
+
+function struncate(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char}, mode::Symbol = :center, prefer_left::Bool = true)","path":"base/strings/util.jl","commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","original_commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","user":{"login":"stevengj","id":2913679,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTM2Nzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2913679?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj","html_url":"https://github.com/stevengj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion

     r == 0 ? stringfn(s, p^q) : stringfn(s, p^q, first(p, r))
 end
 
+"""
+    rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the last characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = rtrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 I lo…"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> rtrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :right)
+
+"""
+    ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the first characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ltrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"…I love 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ltrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`rtrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :left)
+
+"""
+    ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true)
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the middle characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…". By default, the truncation
+prefers keeping chars on the left, but this can be changed by setting `prefer_left` to `false`.
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ctrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 …e 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ctrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`rtrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true) =
+    struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :center, prefer_left)
+
+function struncate(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char}, mode::Symbol = :center, prefer_left::Bool = true)","path":"base/strings/util.jl","commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","original_commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","user":{"login":"stevengj","id":2913679,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTM2Nzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2913679?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj","html_url":"https://github.com/stevengj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"> Regarding mode, IMO something like an enum would be preferable to just Symbol, due to being typo-proof.

Another possibility would be singleton types. {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1705726381","pull_request_review_id":2221616581,"id":1705726381,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5lq1Gt","diff_hunk":"@@ -513,6 +513,101 @@ function rpad(
     r == 0 ? stringfn(s, p^q) : stringfn(s, p^q, first(p, r))
 end
 
+"""
+    rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the last characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = rtrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 I lo…"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> rtrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :right)
+
+"""
+    ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the first characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ltrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"…I love 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ltrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`rtrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :left)
+
+"""
+    ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true)
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the middle characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…". By default, the truncation
+prefers keeping chars on the left, but this can be changed by setting `prefer_left` to `false`.
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ctrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 …e 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ctrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`rtrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true) =
+    struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :center, prefer_left)
+
+function struncate(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char}, mode::Symbol = :center, prefer_left::Bool = true)
+    maxwidth >= 0 || throw(ArgumentError("maxwidth $maxwidth should be non-negative"))

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1705733853","pull_request_review_id":2221629924,"id":1705733853,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5lq27d","diff_hunk":"@@ -513,6 +513,101 @@ function rpad(
     r == 0 ? stringfn(s, p^q) : stringfn(s, p^q, first(p, r))
 end
 
+"""
+    rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the last characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = rtrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 I lo…"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> rtrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :right)
+
+"""
+    ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the first characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ltrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"…I love 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ltrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`rtrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :left)
+
+"""
+    ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true)
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the middle characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…". By default, the truncation
+prefers keeping chars on the left, but this can be changed by setting `prefer_left` to `false`.
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ctrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 …e 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ctrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`rtrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true) =
+    struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :center, prefer_left)
+
+function struncate(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char}, mode::Symbol = :center, prefer_left::Bool = true)
+    maxwidth >= 0 || throw(ArgumentError("maxwidth $maxwidth should be non-negative"))
+    textwidth(str) <= maxwidth && return str","path":"base/strings/util.jl","commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","original_commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","user":{"login":"stevengj","id":2913679,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTM2Nzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2913679?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj","html_url":"https://github.com/stevengj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Also, `textwidth(str) <= maxwidth` is suboptimal because it traverses the entire string even if it is much longer than maxwidth.   Seems better to do something like:
```jl
total_width = 0
for c in str
    total_width += textwidth(c)
    total_width > maxwidth && break
end
total_width <= maxwidth && return string(str)
```

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1705741099","pull_request_review_id":2221641803,"id":1705741099,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5lq4sr","diff_hunk":"@@ -513,6 +513,101 @@ function rpad(
     r == 0 ? stringfn(s, p^q) : stringfn(s, p^q, first(p, r))
 end
 
+"""
+    rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the last characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = rtrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 I lo…"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> rtrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :right)
+
+"""
+    ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the first characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ltrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"…I love 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ltrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`rtrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :left)
+
+"""
+    ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true)
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the middle characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…". By default, the truncation
+prefers keeping chars on the left, but this can be changed by setting `prefer_left` to `false`.
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ctrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 …e 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ctrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`rtrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true) =
+    struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :center, prefer_left)
+
+function struncate(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char}, mode::Symbol = :center, prefer_left::Bool = true)","path":"base/strings/util.jl","commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","original_commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","user":{"login":"stevengj","id":2913679,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTM2Nzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2913679?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj","html_url":"https://github.com/stevengj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"> This should be backed up by some benchmark in my opinion. A single pointer equality check seems to me insignificant from the work of the function.

I tried this with
```jl
s = randstring(10^5)
@btime struncate($s, 100, '…', :center);
```
and I got about an 8% speedup by hard-coding the `mode` checks.  (This is *after* doing the `total_width` optimization noted below — otherwise, it spends all its time on the `textwidth(str)` check.)

     r == 0 ? stringfn(s, p^q) : stringfn(s, p^q, first(p, r))
 end
 
+"""
+    rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the last characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = rtrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 I lo…"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> rtrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+rtrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :right)
+
+"""
+    ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replace_str::AbstractString = "…")
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the first characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…".
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ltrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"…I love 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ltrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`rtrunc`](@ref) and [`ctrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ltrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…') = struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :left)
+
+"""
+    ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true)
+
+Truncate `str` to at most `maxwidth` columns (as estimated by [`textwidth`](@ref)), replacing the middle characters
+with `replacement` if necessary. The default replacement string is "…". By default, the truncation
+prefers keeping chars on the left, but this can be changed by setting `prefer_left` to `false`.
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> s = ctrunc("🍕🍕 I love 🍕", 10)
+"🍕🍕 …e 🍕"
+
+julia> textwidth(s)
+10
+
+julia> ctrunc("foo", 3)
+"foo"
+```
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.12"
+    This function was added in Julia 1.12.
+
+See also [`ltrunc`](@ref) and [`rtrunc`](@ref).
+"""
+ctrunc(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char} = '…'; prefer_left::Bool = true) =
+    struncate(str, maxwidth, replacement, :center, prefer_left)
+
+function struncate(str::AbstractString, maxwidth::Int, replacement::Union{AbstractString,Char}, mode::Symbol = :center, prefer_left::Bool = true)","path":"base/strings/util.jl","commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","original_commit_id":"8436ea3b1905c36e9cae770471be914001293bc5","user":{"login":"stevengj","id":2913679,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTM2Nzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2913679?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj","html_url":"https://github.com/stevengj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"I get another 60% speedup by replacing `textwidth(::Char)` with an optimized version that has a fast path for ASCII characters.  We might want to do this in a separate PR:
```jl
const textwidth_ascii = Cint[textwidth(Char(i)) for i = 0:127]
function textwidth2(c::AbstractChar)
    b = bswap(reinterpret(UInt32, c))
    b < 0x80 && @inbounds return Int(textwidth_ascii[b+1]) # ASCII fast path
    Base.ismalformed(c) && return 1
    Int(ccall(:utf8proc_charwidth, Cint, (UInt32,), c))
end
```","created_at":"2024-08-06T15:33:05Z","updated_at":"2024-08-06T15:33:06Z","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55351#discussion_r1705746669","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55351","author_association":"MEMBER","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1705746669"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55351#discussion_r1705746669"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55351"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1705746669/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":null,"start_side":null,"line":593,"original_line":593,"side":"RIGHT","in_reply_to_id":1705453154,"original_position":81,"position":81,"subject_type":"line"},"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55351","id":2001285035,"node_id":"PR_kwDOABkWpM53SS-r","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55351","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55351.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55351.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55351","number":55351,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"add the common case of ASCII characters, I found in https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55351/files#r1705746669 that `textwidth` can be sped up by 60% or more by adding a fast-path ASCII lookup table.

The slowdown for non-ASCII characters seems negligible (1% or less).  Here, `textwidth2` is the new function, and `textwidth` is the old function (Julia 1.10.4, Apple M1 Pro):
```jl
julia> @btime textwidth('x'); @btime textwidth2('x')
  3.208 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  1.958 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
1

julia> @btime textwidth('α'); @btime textwidth2('α')
  3.208 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
  3.167 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
1
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55398/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55398/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2271633684","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55398#issuecomment-2271633684","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55398","id":2271633684,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6HZmEU","user":{"login":"stevengj","id":2913679,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI5MTM2Nzk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2913679?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj","html_url":"https://github.com/stevengj","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevengj/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-06T15:59:55Z","updated_at":"2024-08-06T15:59:55Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"@jakobnissen, good catch, a tuple table indeed seems to be faster:
